meta.title: Participle Clauses
meta.description: Learn to use present and past participle clauses to create complex, elegant sentences.
meta.description_ko: 현재분사절과 과거분사절을 사용하여 복잡하고 세련된 문장을 만드는 방법을 학습합니다.

explanation.en: Present participle clauses can express time, reason, result, or simultaneous actions.
explanation.ko: 현재분사절은 시간, 이유, 결과, 또는 동시 행동을 표현할 수 있습니다.

questions.prompt: 현재분사절(present participle clause)이 나타낼 수 있는 것은?

explanation.en: 'Reading the report' = simultaneous action (while reading).
explanation.ko: 'Reading the report' = 동시 행동(읽는 동안).

questions.prompt: _____ the report, she noticed several errors. (보고서를 읽으면서 그녀는 몇 가지 오류를 발견했습니다.)

explanation.en: 'Having + past participle' = action completed before the main action.
explanation.ko: 'having + 과거분사' = 주요 행동 전에 완료된 행동.

questions.prompt: 'Having finished his work, he went for a walk.' — 이 문장에서 'Having finished'가 의미하는 것은?

explanation.en: Past participle clause: expresses the reason why she fell asleep.
explanation.ko: 과거분사절: 그녀가 잠든 이유를 표현합니다.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: Exhausted by the long journey, she fell asleep immediately.

explanation.en: Past participle in passive meaning: 'Built in 1889' = 'which was built in 1889'.
explanation.ko: 수동 의미의 과거분사: 'Built in 1889' = '1889년에 건설된'.

questions.prompt: _____ in 1889, the Eiffel Tower is now a global icon. (1889년에 건설된 에펠탑은 이제 세계적인 상징입니다.)

explanation.en: 'Not knowing what to do' = because she didn't know what to do (reason).
explanation.ko: 'Not knowing what to do' = 무엇을 해야 할지 몰랐기 때문에(이유).

questions.prompt: 'Not knowing what to do, she called her manager.'에서 'Not knowing'은 무엇을 나타내나요?

explanation.en: 'Having lived' = perfect participle for a completed past experience that explains the present ability.
explanation.ko: 'having lived' = 현재 능력을 설명하는 완료된 과거 경험을 나타내는 완료 분사.

questions.prompt: _____ lived in France for many years, she speaks French fluently. (프랑스에서 수년간 살았기 때문에 그녀는 프랑스어를 유창하게 합니다.)

explanation.en: Dangling participle: the subject of 'walking' must be the subject of the main clause. 'It started to rain' (not 'the rain').
explanation.ko: 현수 분사: 'walking'의 주어는 주절의 주어와 같아야 합니다.

questions.prompt: 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'Walking down the street, the rain started to fall.'
choices: the rain started → it started to rain

explanation.en: 'Surrounded by enemies' = describes the circumstances/situation.
explanation.ko: 'Surrounded by enemies' = 상황/환경을 설명합니다.

questions.prompt: 'Surrounded by enemies, the general refused to surrender.' — 'Surrounded'의 역할은?

explanation.en: 'Knowing the risks' = because they knew the risks (reason clause).
explanation.ko: 'Knowing the risks' = 위험을 알았기 때문에(이유절).

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: Knowing the risks involved, they decided to proceed.

explanation.en: Past participle 'led' = passive meaning (the project was led by experts).
explanation.ko: 과거분사 'led' = 수동 의미(프로젝트가 전문가들에 의해 이끌어졌다).

questions.prompt: The project, _____ by a team of experts, was completed ahead of schedule. (전문가 팀에 의해 이끌어진 그 프로젝트는 예정보다 일찍 완료됐습니다.)

explanation.en: Participle clauses make writing more concise and elegant by avoiding repetition.
explanation.ko: 분사절은 반복을 피하여 글을 더 간결하고 세련되게 만듭니다.

questions.prompt: 분사절을 사용하는 주된 이유는?
choices: 문장을 더 유창하고 경제적으로 만들기 위해

explanation.en: 'Wanting to go' = present participle describing the children's state.
explanation.ko: 'wanting to go' = 아이들의 상태를 묘사하는 현재분사.

questions.prompt: The children, _____ to go to the party, were disappointed when it was cancelled. (파티에 가고 싶어했던 아이들은 취소됐을 때 실망했습니다.)

explanation.en: 'Having considered all options' = after considering all options (completed action before main verb).
explanation.ko: 'Having considered all options' = 모든 선택지를 고려한 후(주동사 전 완료된 행동).

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: Having considered all options, she chose the safest one.

explanation.en: 'Examined by the lawyer' = passive past participle (the document was examined).
explanation.ko: 'examined by the lawyer' = 수동 과거분사(문서가 검토되었다).

questions.prompt: The document, _____ carefully by the lawyer, revealed hidden clauses. (변호사에 의해 주의 깊게 검토된 그 문서는 숨겨진 조항을 드러냈습니다.)

explanation.en: 'Having been exhausted' is grammatically possible but overly complex. 'Exhausted' or 'Being exhausted' is more natural.
explanation.ko: 'Having been exhausted'는 문법적으로 가능하지만 지나치게 복잡합니다. 'Exhausted'나 'Being exhausted'가 더 자연스럽습니다.

questions.prompt: 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'Having been exhausted, she went to bed early.'
choices: Having been exhausted → Exhausted / Being exhausted
went to bed → went to sleep

explanation.en: 'Taking a shortcut' = by taking a shortcut (method/manner).
explanation.ko: 'Taking a shortcut' = 지름길을 통해(방법/방식).

questions.prompt: _____ a shortcut through the park, he arrived earlier than expected. (공원을 통해 지름길로 가서 그는 예상보다 일찍 도착했습니다.)

explanation.en: 'Though promising' = 'though they are promising' — the subject + be verb are omitted.
explanation.ko: 'though promising' = 'though they are promising' — 주어 + be 동사가 생략됨.

questions.prompt: 'The results, though promising, require further investigation.' — 이 문장에서 생략된 것은?

explanation.en: 'Given + noun phrase' = considering / taking into account.
explanation.ko: 'given + 명사구' = 고려하면 / 감안하면.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: Given the current economic situation, redundancies were inevitable.

explanation.en: 'With + noun + past participle/adjective' = absolute clause expressing circumstances.
explanation.ko: 'with + 명사 + 과거분사/형용사' = 상황을 표현하는 독립 분사 구문.

questions.prompt: _____ no other option available, they were forced to accept the terms. (다른 선택지가 없어서 그들은 조건을 수락할 수밖에 없었습니다.)

explanation.en: 'Smiling broadly' = simultaneous action (he was smiling while accepting the award).
explanation.ko: 'Smiling broadly' = 동시 행동(상을 받는 동안 웃고 있었다).

questions.prompt: 'Smiling broadly, he accepted the award.' — 이 분사절이 나타내는 것은?

explanation.en: 'Badly damaged by the storm' = past passive participle expressing the building's state.
explanation.ko: 'Badly damaged by the storm' = 건물의 상태를 나타내는 과거 수동 분사.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: Badly damaged by the storm, the building was quickly repaired.

explanation.en: 'Having made a breakthrough' = perfect participle for action completed before publishing.
explanation.ko: 'having made a breakthrough' = 발표 전에 완료된 행동을 나타내는 완료 분사.

questions.prompt: The scientist, _____ a major breakthrough, published her findings immediately. (중요한 돌파구를 만들고 나서 그 과학자는 즉시 연구 결과를 발표했습니다.)

explanation.en: 'Inspired by' = passive past participle (they were inspired by the lecture).
explanation.ko: 'inspired by' = 수동 과거분사(그들은 강의에 의해 영감을 받았다).

questions.prompt: 빈칸에 알맞은 것은? '_____ by the professor's lecture, the students stayed behind to ask questions.'
